commands.review_summary:
The PR clamps out-of-range WhatsApp timestamp conversions at import and archive-read boundaries and adds regression tests for chats/unread and status JSON output.

Reproducibility: yes. The PR body gives a real archive reproduction for --json chats/unread on current main, and source inspection shows current main passes out-of-range stored seconds through time.Unix into JSON-facing time.Time values.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed because the current PR head has no blocking review finding to fix automatically.

commands.author: rmorgans
commands.command: re-review — addressed the p2 status path. `store.status` now routes `oldestmessage`/`newestmessage` through the same `fromunix` clamp instead of bare `time.unix`, so a poisoned `messages.ts` can't break `--json status` either. added `teststatusclampsoutofrangemessagetimestamp`, and corrected the pr body: the live sentinel was in `chats` (which is why `status` tolerated it pre-fix), so the `status`/`messages.ts` case is covered by the regression test rather than the live proof. commit 121177b
